phases.name: html
phases.prompt: Write 'index.html' for a browser Snake game split across several JS modules. It must be a complete HTML5 document that links 'styles.css', contains a <canvas id="board" width="400" height="400"></canvas> and a score display with id="score", and at the end of <body> loads ES modules with a single <script type="module" src="main.js"></script>. Output ONLY the complete contents of index.html inside a single ```html code block, nothing else.

phases.name: css
constants
phases.prompt: Write 'constants.js', an ES module that exports game constants for a canvas Snake game: CELL_SIZE (e.g. 20), GRID_SIZE (e.g. 20), and TICK_MS (e.g. 120). Use named `export const` declarations. Output ONLY the complete contents of constants.js inside a single ```js code block, nothing else.

phases.name: board
snake
food
input
game
main

scorers.rubric: main.js is a valid ES-module entry point that obtains the #board canvas context and #score element from the provided index.html, imports and starts the Game from game.js, wires attachInput from input.js, and starts the loop, consistent with the exported names of those modules.

phases.name: readme
phases.prompt:
Here is the project structure: index.html loads main.js (ES module) which uses game.js, snake.js, food.js, board.js, input.js, and constants.js, styled by styles.css.

index.html:

{{.Out.html}}

Write a 'README.md' documenting this modular browser Snake game: a title, a short description, a list of the source files and what each does, the controls, and how to run it (it uses ES modules, so it must be served over http, not opened as a file:// URL). Output ONLY the complete contents of README.md inside a single ```markdown code block, nothing else.
